slices.focus: Paper scorekeeping at an Astros game as mental reset
slices.summary: Spontaneously went to an Astros game with coworker Miguel after a late meeting. He taught simple paper scorekeeping — a structured, bounded activity that held attention and kept work thoughts away for three hours. Realized genuine rest comes from low-pressure social connection, tactile focus, and a clear endpoint.
open_loops: Whether a second game will feel as grounding as the first
Whether bounded activities with a built-in frame can apply to other areas beyond baseball
Whether the pencil in the truck will actually turn into a second scorecard
decisions: Keep this first scorecard as a meaningful marker of a restorative evening
Keep a simple pencil in the truck for spontaneous future games
Go to games only when already downtown, max once or twice a season
